calendar.matters:
Trustees Petition For Settlement Of Her First Interim Account; Petition For Instructions (Allocation Of Trust Estate To Subtrusts; Authorizing Trustee Not To Seek Confirmation From The Beneficiaries Of One Subtrust That They Will Not Renounce The Beneficiaries Interest As Requested By Settlor Of The Other Subtrust) And For Orders Terminating And Directing Distribution Of Subtrusts
Off Calendar Jun-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw for and with Carolyn Espey Silverman. Francesca Callejo, proposed Guardian ad Litem (GAL).

The Court stated that we are here for a number of requests, one of which is the allocation of assets into the subtrusts and that the original petition alleged that the assets were already in the subtrusts. Ms. Espey reported that the assets have been and continue to be held in the Slavoj Family Trust and not in the subtrusts. The Court stated that there is a request that the trustee not follow the trusts instructions and asked why they would not just ask the beneficiaries. Ms. Hinshaw reported that it is expensive to communicate with the beneficiaries, everything must be translated into two languages and the cost of postage is very high, stating that the last mailing cost $1600.00. The Court stated that they need to honor the trustors wishes, that the confirmation from the beneficiaries could have been sent with the notice of this hearing, that the direction is to distribute to everyone regardless of their status of education, that the point of the trust is to support education, that it is the trustees job to prioritize the payments and asks why the trustors desires should be thrown away. Ms. Hinshaw stated that the trustor assumed that the 2 pieces of real property would be generating income, that the properties were sold and that the income generating from the cash is not enough money to make a difference. The Court stated that it could make a difference in Slovakia and asked if a GAL was needed. Ms. Callejo stated that she is not currently acting as GAL, that she was told there was a number of minor heirs, that she does not know to what extent the trustee has received names and that she is available to act as GAL. Ms. Espey stated that a GAL is not necessary if there are no distributions. The Court stated that the evidence before the court is that there is $2500 to $3000 per year available, that it does not have a request to spend principle on scholarships before it, that it will not appoint a GAL as the instructions in the trust will be followed and asked about the status of the undeposited checks. Ms. Espey stated that the issue has been resolved and the checks have been deposited. The Court stated that the accounting will be allowed not approved due to the issue that it covers the time of the prior trustee and requested Ms. Hinshaw to prepare a revised order. The Trustees Petition for Settlement of Her First Interim Account, etc., was granted (subject to receipt/review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputies Lopes & Santiago. (Not Reported)

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee; For Order Setting Bond; An For Order Modifying Trust Provisions Related To Trustee Fee
Off Calendar Apr-02-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Espey-Silverman for and with Irene Novack.

The Court noted that the only issue before the Court is the amount of the bond. The Court ordered bond in the amount of $928,000.00 for Vera's Family Trust and $928,000.00 for Desider's Restatement Trust. The request to change the fee schedule to 1% is approved but not the extraordinary fees. The successor Trustee will take action to appoint an outside US trustee. The Court granted the Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee; For Order Setting Bond; An For Order Modifying Trust Provisions Related To Trustee Fee
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Continued To Mar-27-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee; For Order Setting Bond; An For Order Modifying Trust Provisions Related To Trustee Fee
Off Calendar Feb-27-2013 Continued To Mar-13-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee; For Order Setting Bond; An For Order Modifying Trust Provisions Related To Trustee Fee
Off Calendar Jan-16-2013 Continued To Feb-27-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Espy Silverman for and with Irene Novak (Petitioner).

The Court informed Counsel and Ms. Novak that they need to make another effort to have a Successor Trustee appointed in Vera's trust out of the three individuals and the nine entities and that they need to get a Co-Trustee out of the US to serve in Desider's Trust. Ms. Espy stated that Dr. Lenka Holikova said that will act as Co-Trustee. Ms. Novak explained the reasons why she can no longer serve as Co-Trustee. Ms. Espy also stated that everyone who was named has declined to act (Novak, Nosek, Zak and Bank of America all declined). The Court also addressed language suggested by Counsel with regard to the 1% guideline fee. The Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee, etc. was continued to February 27, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Espy to contact all of the named Beneficiaries of both Sub-Trusts to see if they can come up with a nominee.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
